ai_strategy.summary: North Carolina State University's public AI posture combines published guidance, teaching resources, research centers, AI-relevant programs, and research computing. Public evidence points to distributed governance rather than a verified standalone chief AI officer.

key_provisions: Use appropriate or approved AI tools within institutional data rules
Faculty should set clear course-level AI expectations
Users remain responsible for accuracy, attribution, privacy, and academic integrity
Sensitive data should not be entered into unapproved public tools

regulatory_context.summary: North Carolina State University operates under federal education, privacy, and research obligations plus University of North Carolina system rules. This sweep found institutional AI guidance but no separate campus-specific AI statute.

narrative_summary: North Carolina State University is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is visible in responsible-use guidance, classroom resources, AI-relevant research structures, credentials or workforce programming, and research computing. The campus permits AI experimentation with guardrails rather than presenting a simple prohibition. Faculty-facing materials make course policy and assessment design central to implementation, while technology guidance emphasizes privacy, security, attribution, and responsible use. Research evidence comes from AI, machine-learning, data-science, policy, health, autonomy, or computing organizations, supported by high-performance or research computing resources. The profile is strongest on public policy resources, research centers, programs, and infrastructure, and weaker on exact budgets, enterprise license counts, adoption metrics, and named executive ownership. Overall, the institution appears to be in a growing-to-operational phase: AI work is real and distributed across functions, but public evidence does not yet show a single centralized AI operating model with disclosed outcomes.

gaps_acknowledged: Current president, provost, CIO, and chief AI officer were not independently verified in this worker sweep, so leadership names are left null.
Exact enterprise AI contract values, user counts, and adoption metrics were not publicly confirmed.
A $20 million NSF AI Institute award is public, but no single total AI budget or campus enterprise AI spend was disclosed in the public sources used.
NC State's Hazel HPC service is public, but the reviewed pages did not disclose an exact AI GPU count.
No institution-specific AI litigation or major controversy was confirmed.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
